commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
The prophecy. Singing praises to God is here called prophecy: the more, because these singers were often inspired men. (Challoner)
Hebrew Massa signifies a song, (Haydock) elevation, (Calmet) burden. (Syriac) So the Vulgate has onus Moab, the burden or dreadful prediction against Moab. (Calmet)
Prophecy often denotes no more than a melodious canticle, 1 Kings x. 10. (Haydock)
Chonenias directed all this music. Septuagint simply style him "prince of the Levites, prince of the musicians "(Calmet) or archon ton odon (who gave out the songs) "because he was intelligent. "(Haydock)

commentaries.author: Richard Challoner
commentaries.content: The prophecy, to give out the tunes: Singing praises to God is here called prophecy: the more, because these singers were often inspired men.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
